metadata.subject: English Language Arts
metadata.description: New Media 11 explores the evolving landscape of digital communication, media production, and critical media literacy. Students analyze how new media platforms shape culture, identity, and power, and develop skills in creating purposeful digital texts — including video, audio, web content, and social media. The course connects media literacy to civic engagement, Indigenous digital sovereignty, and ethical communication. Students develop as both critical consumers and skilled producers of new media.
metadata.general_description: A course developing critical media literacy, digital text creation, and ethical digital communication — exploring how new media shapes identity, culture, and power in the digital age.

big_ideas.statement: New media shapes and is shaped by culture, identity, and power.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students examine the reciprocal relationship between digital platforms and social life — how algorithms, platforms, and media ecosystems influence what we believe, how we see ourselves, and who has power.

big_ideas.statement: Effective digital communication requires understanding purpose, audience, and platform.
big_ideas.elaboration: Grade 11 students develop the ability to make purposeful choices about media form, platform affordances, and audience needs when creating digital texts.

big_ideas.statement: Critical media literacy is essential for navigating the digital world with agency.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students develop the analytical skills to evaluate digital content for accuracy, bias, source credibility, algorithmic influence, and commercial intent.

big_ideas.statement: Digital media can be a powerful tool for storytelling, advocacy, and community building.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students explore how Indigenous communities and social movements use digital media for cultural preservation, political advocacy, and community connection.

competencies.raw: Analyze and evaluate digital and media texts for purpose, audience, and effect; identify how new media constructs meaning, identity, and power; apply critical media literacy frameworks to diverse digital texts.
i_can_statements: I can analyze digital and media texts using critical frameworks — identifying rhetorical strategies, bias, and purpose.
I can evaluate how algorithms, platform design, and economic models shape what we see and believe online.
I can identify how digital media constructs and challenges representations of identity, culture, and power.
I can connect Indigenous digital sovereignty and media self-representation to broader questions of justice.

competencies.raw: Create purposeful and technically proficient new media texts for specific audiences and platforms; apply ethical and responsible practices in digital creation and sharing; reflect on the social impact and responsibilities of digital media production.
i_can_statements: I can create polished digital texts — video, audio, web, social media — tailored to specific purposes and audiences.
I can apply ethical practices in digital creation, including attribution, consent, privacy, and responsible sharing.
I can use technical skills in production tools to communicate ideas effectively through digital media.
I can reflect on the social impact of my digital creations and take responsibility as a media producer.

areas.name: Critical Media Literacy
areas.raw: Analyzing new media texts, platforms, and digital culture.
topics: Media literacy frameworks: SMART, CRAAP, and other analytical tools for evaluating digital content
Misinformation and disinformation: identifying false information, deepfakes, and manipulated media
Algorithmic culture: how recommendation systems and filter bubbles shape exposure and belief
Surveillance capitalism: data collection, privacy, and the economics of attention
Representation in media: how digital media constructs race, gender, class, and other identities
Indigenous digital sovereignty: First Peoples' rights to control their digital representation and data

areas.name: Digital Storytelling and Video Production
areas.raw: Video production, documentary, and digital narrative.
topics: Narrative structure in video: adapting story structure to short and long-form digital formats
Pre-production: scripting, storyboarding, shot lists, and production planning
Camera technique: composition, lighting, sound recording, and visual storytelling
Editing: non-linear editing principles, pacing, transitions, and post-production workflows
Documentary and journalism: research, interview, and ethical issues in non-fiction digital media
Short film and video essay production

areas.name: Audio and Podcast Production
areas.raw: Podcast creation, audio storytelling, and sound design.
topics: Podcast formats: interview, narrative, debate, educational, and hybrid formats
Audio recording and editing: microphone technique, Audacity/GarageBand, and sound quality
Sound design: music, sound effects, and ambient sound as narrative tools
Voice and delivery: pacing, tone, and clarity in audio performance
Research and scripting for audio journalism and narrative podcast
Publishing and distributing podcast content

areas.name: Web and Social Media Communication
areas.raw: Web design, social media strategy, and digital civic communication.
topics: Web communication: principles of effective web writing, UX, and accessible design
Social media platforms: affordances, audiences, and communication norms across platforms
Digital activism and advocacy: using social media for social justice and community action
Personal branding and digital identity: curating an authentic and purposeful online presence
Copyright, Creative Commons, and ethical content use online
Community building through digital media: connecting with audiences and building networks

areas.name: Digital Ethics and Civic Responsibility
areas.raw: Ethics, privacy, digital citizenship, and responsible media production.
topics: Digital citizenship: rights and responsibilities in online communities
Online safety and privacy: protecting personal information and navigating digital risks
Cyberbullying, hate speech, and online harassment: recognizing, responding, and preventing
Consent in digital media: permissions, image rights, and ethical filming/recording
Environmental impact of digital technology: data centres, e-waste, and digital sustainability
Developing a personal digital ethics framework
